title: Search suggestions vs autocomplete
summary: Use search suggestions to guide query formulation. Use autocomplete when the user must complete or choose a known value.
decisionRules: Prefer search suggestions when several query paths may be useful.
Prefer autocomplete for constrained values such as addresses, products, people, or codes.
Keep typed input editable and never force an irrelevant suggestion.
failureModes: Submitting a suggestion before the user confirms it.
Using autocomplete where free-form search is expected.
Showing stale suggestions that do not match the current input.

sources.note: Carbon search guidance covers recent and type-ahead search suggestions for query formulation.
Ontario autocomplete guidance supports the constrained known-value autocomplete side of the comparison.
APG combobox guidance documents autocomplete behavior, popup suggestions, and keyboard expectations.
